display.purpose: Large page headlines and rare first-viewport statements.

headline.purpose: Major section ideas such as the map introduction.

title.purpose: Trail heads, group titles, and article section heads.

body.purpose: Default interface copy and readable content.

label.purpose: Eyebrows, metadata, dates, and compact annotations.

shadows.name: lift
shadows.purpose: Rare raised-surface or interactive emphasis shadow.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default state transitions and quiet UI response.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: More pronounced reveal motion when structure is being exposed.

components.name: Menu Toggle
components.description: Compact ghost button for mobile navigation.
components.css: .ds-menu-toggle { font: inherit; font-size: var(--text-sm, 0.88rem); font-weight: 600; background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--color-hairline, oklch(0.852 0.02 132)); border-radius: var(--radius-md, 6px); padding: 0.4rem 0.85rem; color: var(--color-ink, oklch(0.3 0.022 155)); cursor: pointer; } .ds-menu-toggle:hover { background: var(--color-sage, oklch(0.922 0.022 135)); } .ds-menu-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Trail Link
components.description: Evidence-trail link for writing, projects, contact channels, and external routes.
components.css: .ds-trail-link { color: var(--color-river-deep, oklch(0.438 0.072 224)); font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1.5px solid oklch(0.52 0.078 222 / 0.35); transition: border-color 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-trail-link:hover { border-bottom-color: currentColor; } .ds-trail-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Text-plus-dot status marker that never relies on color alone.
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4em; font-size: var(--text-xs, 0.78rem); font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; color: var(--color-ink-soft, oklch(0.452 0.021 154)); } .ds-status-dot { width: 0.5em; height: 0.5em; border-radius: 99px; background: currentColor; } .ds-status-active { color: var(--color-moss-deep, oklch(0.424 0.078 152)); }

components.name: Workbench Row
components.description: A row-based project item, deliberately not a card.
components.html: PromptPalA small system around reusable AI prompts.active · 2026
components.css: .ds-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(9rem, 14rem) 1fr auto; align-items: baseline; gap: 0.4rem 2rem; padding: 1.35rem 0.5rem; text-decoration: none; color: var(--color-ink, oklch(0.3 0.022 155)); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--color-hairline, oklch(0.852 0.02 132)); transition: background 0.18s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-row:hover { background: var(--color-sage, oklch(0.922 0.022 135)); } .ds-row-name { font-size: var(--text-lg, 1.2rem); font-weight: 700; } .ds-row:hover .ds-row-name { color: var(--color-moss-deep, oklch(0.424 0.078 152)); } .ds-row-intent { color: var(--color-ink-soft, oklch(0.452 0.021 154)); } .ds-row-meta { font-size: var(--text-sm, 0.88rem); color: var(--color-ink-faint, oklch(0.572 0.019 150)); white-space: nowrap; } .ds-row:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Constellation Node
components.description: Thread selector for the systems map.
components.css: .ds-node { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.55rem; width: 10rem; background: none; border: 0; cursor: pointer; font: inherit; color: var(--color-ink-soft, oklch(0.452 0.021 154)); } .ds-node-dot { width: 14px; height: 14px; border-radius: 99px; background: var(--color-paper, oklch(0.968 0.013 95)); border: 2px solid var(--color-sage-deep, oklch(0.886 0.026 138)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 7px var(--color-paper, oklch(0.968 0.013 95)); transition: border-color 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), background 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-node-label { font-size: var(--text-sm, 0.88rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-node:hover .ds-node-dot, .ds-node:focus-visible .ds-node-dot { border-color: var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); } .ds-node[aria-pressed='true'] .ds-node-dot { background: var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); border-color: var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); transform: scale(1.18); } .ds-node[aria-pressed='true'] .ds-node-label { color: var(--color-ink, oklch(0.3 0.022 155)); } .ds-node:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-moss, oklch(0.52 0.087 150)); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Code Block
components.description: Forest-ground code surface for long-form writing.
components.html: notice friction; build the small system around it
components.css: .ds-code { background: var(--color-forest, oklch(0.302 0.038 158)); color: var(--color-forest-ink, oklch(0.93 0.016 100)); padding: 1.1rem 1.25rem; border-radius: var(--radius-md, 6px); overflow-x: auto; font-size: 0.86rem; line-height: 1.6; font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; }

narrative.overview:
This site should feel like a well-kept workshop near water: calm, structured, useful, and personal. The interface is a map of recurring threads, not a resume page wearing nicer typography. Visitors should sense practical credibility first, then find evidence trails into writing, projects, roles, references, and experiments.

The system uses warm paper, muted living greens, river blues, precise sans-serif type, and mostly flat surfaces. Structure comes from rules, lines, typographic hierarchy, and row-based indexes rather than decorative cards. The craft reference is Braun and Nomos restraint translated into a personal systems map.

It explicitly rejects generic portfolio hero sections, inflated AI and innovation language, corporate resume templates, LinkedIn reformatted into cards, consultant gloss, shallow futurism, neon AI motifs, and decorative hacker styling.
keyCharacteristics: Calm map-first structure with visible evidence trails.
Committed green identity on warm, never-white paper.
Dense but readable indexes, especially for Workbench and Writing.
Quiet interaction: line drawing, reveal, routing, hover emphasis.
Maker signal through structure and project archaeology, not tech decoration.

rules.name: The Committed Green Rule
rules.body: Moss and sage carry the identity; do not replace them with generic blue, purple, or grayscale portfolio defaults.

rules.name: The Evidence Trail Rule
rules.body: River blue means followable path. Do not use it for passive decoration.

rules.name: The No Pure Extremes Rule
rules.body: Pure white and pure black are forbidden. Every neutral is tinted toward the green-paper world.

rules.name: The Sans-Led Rule
rules.body: Do not use a display serif to make the site feel premium. The brand should feel precise and humane, not over-editorial.

rules.name: The Real Mono Rule
rules.body: Monospace is allowed only for code or terminal affordances. It is not shorthand for technical credibility.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. If the design needs hierarchy, use spacing, typography, dividers, or tonal paper before adding shadow.

rules.name: The No Glass Rule
rules.body: Decorative blur, translucent glass cards, and frosted panels are prohibited.

dos: Do lead with plain credibility and let the evidence trails carry depth.
Do keep the site structured like a thoughtful map of Jin's work, learning, writing, and influences.
Do use moss for active structure and river for followable links.
Do preserve readable long-form writing with a 65-75ch measure.
Do show maker signal through Workbench archaeology, GitHub references, experiments, and concrete project context.
Do keep all status and category cues readable without color.
Do respect reduced-motion users; animation may reveal structure but must never contain the only copy of information.
donts: Don't build generic portfolio hero sections.
Don't turn the site into LinkedIn reformatted into cards.
Don't use inflated AI or innovation language.
Don't use corporate resume templates, consultant gloss, or shallow futurism.
Don't use neon AI motifs, decorative hacker aesthetics, or decorative tech motifs that do not reveal how Jin thinks or works.
Don't create repeated identical card grids that treat every project as the same kind of object.
Don't use pure white, pure black, gradient text, glassmorphism, or side-stripe borders greater than 1px.
